calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Execution Of Writ Of Mandate Pending Appeal
Off Calendar Mar-08-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AVIVA J. GILBERT, ESQ. of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel No. 415-954-4478 appearing for Petitioner Belinda Skulason;
Deputy Attorney General NHAN VU of the Office of the Attorney General of the State of California, Tel No. 213-897-2484 appearing for Respondent California Bureau of Real Estate.

Matter argued and submitted. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. As to PETITIONER BELINDA SKULASON'S Motion For Execution Of Writ Of Mandate Pending Appeal, Ms. Skulason's motion for an order that the BRE's notice of appeal not stay execution of the writ is granted. The nature of the writ - requiring expunged convictions to be removed from a website and public records - establishes that the failure to execute it will likely result in irreparable damage to Ms. Skulason. To the extent that any additional showing is required by Code of Civil Procedure 1110b, Ms. Skulason has made such a showing. The continued public availability of the convictions is itself a deterrent to Ms. Skulason seeking employment in the real estate field and undermines the value of having the convictions expunged and thus constitutes irreparable damage. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Feb-05-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AVIVA J. GILBERT, ESQ. of Farella Braun & Martel LLP, Tel No. 415-954-4400 appearing for the Petitioner;
Atty. NHAN VU, ESQ. of the State of California Office of the Attorney General, Tel No. 213-897-2484 appearing for the Respondent.

Matter argued and submitted by counsel. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. PETITIONER BELINDA SKULASON'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is granted in large part. Plaintiff is awarded fees and costs of $42,430.19, consisting of $40,000 in fees and $2,430.19 in costs. Plaintiff is entitled to fees per CCP 1021.5. The hourly rates of her attorneys is reasonable, but the Court finds upon review of the court filings that the hours expended, were excessive given the limited factual and legal issues involved and thus the Court has reduced those hours by roughly 35%.The computerized research costs are recoverable as attorney's fees, while the copying and delivery costs are not recoverable. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976 (302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Jan-22-2016 Continued To Feb-05-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

PETITIONER BELINDA SKULASON'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Costs is continued to February 5, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 to give the opposing party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Opposing party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 22, 2016 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be granted. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Not reported. (302/EG)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On Peremptory Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-03-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. AVIVA J. GILBERT OF FARELLA BRAUN & MARTEL LLP, TEL. NO. 1-415-954-4478 APPEARING FOR THE PETITIONER;
ATTY. DAVID LEW, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-510-622-2225 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; PETITIONER BELINDA SKULASON'S Motion For Judgment On Peremptory Writ Of Mandate is Granted. The respondent is ordered to permanently remove the documents detailing Petitioners' expunged conviction from its public website. Prevailing party to prepare a comprehensive form of order stating the law and to comply with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
